016package org.consensusj.bitcoinj.util;
017
018import org.bitcoinj.core.Block;
019import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
020import org.bitcoinj.base.internal.ByteUtils;
021
022import java.math.BigInteger;
023import java.util.Arrays;
024
025/**
026 * Utility class containing {@link #blockHeightFromCoinbase(Block)}
027 */
028public class BlockUtil {
029
030    /**
031     * Return the block height contained in a standalone BIP34-compatible
032     * Bitcoin block (or -1 if it's a pre-BIP34 block)
033     *
034     * @param block A Bitcoin block (need not be attached to a chain)
035     * @return The block height if available (BIP 34) or -1.
036     */
037    public static int blockHeightFromCoinbase(Block block) {
038        if (!block.isBIP34()) {
039            // Must be BIP34-compatible (block  227,836 or higher)
040            return Block.BLOCK_HEIGHT_UNKNOWN;
041        }
042        Transaction coinbase = block.getTransactions().get(0);
043        byte[] scriptBytes = coinbase.getInput(0).getScriptBytes();
044        // There appears to be a bug in bitcoinj 0.15.8 for recent blocks where the input[0] script of the coinbase tx can't be parsed
045        // The below code works for block 324140 but seems to fail on SegWit blocks, due to https://github.com/bitcoinj/bitcoinj/issues/1595
046//        Script script = new Script(scriptBytes);
047//        ScriptChunk chunk = script.getChunks().get(0);
048//        BigInteger height = new BigInteger(Utils.reverseBytes(chunk.data));
049        // Workaround: We'll parse just the bytes we need
050        BigInteger height;
051        if (scriptBytes[0] == 3) {
052            byte[] blockNumBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(scriptBytes, 1, 4);
053            height = new BigInteger(ByteUtils.reverseBytes(blockNumBytes));
054        } else {
055            height = BigInteger.valueOf(-1);
056        }
057        return height.intValueExact();
058    }
059}
